CONTENT.Early life: Davidzada was born in Beersheba, Israel, to a Sephardic Jewish family.
CONTENT.Club career: He made his debut for Hapoel Be'er Sheva against Maccabi Haifa in February 2010. He established himself in the team during the 2010–11 season.On 5 August 2016, it was announced that Davidzada has signed a four-season contract with K.A.A. Gent and would join the team after Hapoel Be'er Sheva's Champions League qualifying play-off matches against Scottish Champions Celtic.On 7 September 2018, Davidzada signed a four-year deal with Maccabi Tel Aviv.
CONTENT.International career: Davidzada was called up to represent Israel at under-21 level. He took part in the 2013 UEFA European Under-21 Football Championship.

SUMMARY: Ofir Davidzada (or Davidadze, Hebrew: אופיר דוידזאדה; born (1991-05-05)5 May 1991) is an Israeli professional footballer who plays as a left back for Maccabi Tel Aviv.
